audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for weakness/trembling, Spirit and power, hidden wisdom, Spirit searching depths, spiritual discernment, mind of Messiah.

verses.restored: And I, coming to you, siblings, did not come according to superiority of word or wisdom, announcing to you the mystery/testimony of the Divine.
For I decided to know nothing among you except Jesus Messiah, and him crucified.
And I was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling.
My word and my proclamation were not in persuasive words of wisdom, but in demonstration of Spirit and power,
so that your trust might not be in human wisdom but in power of the Divine.
Yet we speak wisdom among the mature/complete, but not wisdom of this age nor of rulers of this age who are being made inactive.
But we speak Divine wisdom in mystery, hidden, which the Divine marked out beforehand before the ages for our glory,
which none of the rulers of this age has known; for if they had known, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
But just as it is written: What eye did not see and ear did not hear and did not rise upon the human heart, what the Divine prepared for those loving him.
For to us the Divine unveiled these things through the Spirit; for the Spirit searches all things, even the depths of the Divine.
For who among humans knows the things of a human except the spirit of the human within? So also the things of the Divine no one has known except the Spirit of the Divine.
But we did not receive the spirit of the world, but the Spirit from the Divine, so that we may know the things graciously given to us by the Divine.
These things we also speak, not in taught words of human wisdom, but in taught words of Spirit, interpreting spiritual things to spiritual ones.

But the natural/soul-level human does not receive the things of the Spirit of the Divine, for they are foolishness to that one, and one is not able to know them, because they are spiritually discerned.
The spiritual one discerns all things, but is discerned by no one.
For who has known the mind of the Lord, who will instruct him? But we have the mind of Messiah.

translation_notes: Psychikos is rendered natural/soul-level to preserve the contrast with pneumatikos/spiritual without implying the soul is evil.
Paul rejects status-performance wisdom, not deep wisdom itself; he speaks hidden Divine wisdom through Spirit.
